items.text: Gap mechanic documented from source/state/tests (verbs + CLI surface, gap artifact/schema, create/list/resolve flow, relation to workstreams/plan items) and the review_gaps -> gap-mechanic mapping is recorded via lume decide.

Constraints confirmed against docs/constraints.md and docs/vision.md and recorded via lume decide: no-LLM/no-network deterministic verb, agent does live ecosystem lookup (verb hardcodes/fetches nothing), all dates via the clock seam (plugin/src/lume/clock.py).

Emit/ingest split settled and recorded via lume decide: emit prints protocol + context only; ingest validates, writes the markdown report, persists via the store seam, and emits (never executes) the queue-command plan; neither transitions workstreams or records gaps itself.

Generic charter discovery design recorded via lume decide: PRIMARY lume-state (objectives/decisions/retros/plan), SECONDARY capped pattern scan, --charter glob override, graceful degradation with explicit thin-coverage flag in the emitted protocol.

Output conventions recorded via lume decide: .lume/review-<date-slug>-NN/ folder (clock date, NN next-free zero-padded daily sequence from 01), findings.md as the primary captured record (the one sanctioned raw file write), structured result reusing the discovery artifact shape through the store seam.

Architecture anchors verified against current code (catalog.py registration, handlers/discovery.py + handlers/authoring.py, cli/app.py dispatch, schemas/review_result.json surfaced via lume schema and lume entities) and any divergences from the spec noted in discovery findings.
Discovery artifact produced capturing findings and open forks; no engine/product code changed; items deferred to planning/execution are listed.

dod.preamble: Discovery for the lume review verb: learn the existing gap mechanic before designing anything, confirm the hard constraints, and settle the design forks with recorded decisions. No engine code in this iteration.

handback: Discovery complete for the lume review verb. Produced: schema-valid discovery.json (8 sections: gap mechanic, constraints, emit/ingest split, charter discovery, output conventions, architecture anchors, schema-subset constraint, open forks) and 7 decisions logged via lume decide. Key findings: gaps live under .lume/gaps/<source>-G<n>.json (relocated by child 0016; superseding decision logged) with ids assigned at execution time, so ingest emits 'lume gap add' commands rather than precomputing ids; validate.py is a deliberate JSON Schema subset that review_result.json must fit; reviews are repo-level so the store seam needs a small repo-level extension rather than misfiling under a workstream. Six concrete forks are deferred to planning. No code changed; tests green. Next: a planning iteration to slice execution. Redone after reject: discovery record updated for the .lume/gaps/ relocation.
self_review: Holds: the gap mechanic was learned from source (gap.py, gap.json, handlers/authoring.py, tests) before any design - gaps are repo-root, per-source-sequenced, repo-level records, and review_gaps map to emitted 'lume gap add' commands, never executed by ingest. All seven DoD items are met: constraints confirmed against docs/constraints.md and docs/vision.md (the verb is even anticipated there as a constitutional guardrail), and all five design forks (emit/ingest split, charter precedence, folder/markdown/store conventions, gap mapping, schema-subset constraint) are recorded as seven lume decide entries plus a schema-valid discovery.json. Architecture anchors verified against current code, with one spec divergence noted: handlers live at cli/handlers/, and dispatch is the HANDLERS dict, so app.py likely needs no edit. Weak/honest: the store-seam extension for the repo-level review artifact is decided in direction (TrackingStore gains review addressing) but its exact shape is deferred; same for the repeatable --charter flag helper, the -w-scoping question, and concrete doc-scan caps - all listed as open forks for planning. 'lume get discovery' is not a supported entity fetch, so the artifact was validated directly via validate_entity instead. No engine code was changed; suite green (373 passed). Redo (post-reject): the operator rejected because child workstream 0016 relocated gaps to .lume/gaps/ mid-flight, making the discovery record's location detail stale. Corrected: discovery.json's gap-mechanic section now states .lume/gaps/ (with the relocation noted), and a superseding decision is logged. The review_gaps mapping itself was already location-independent (emitted 'lume gap add' commands resolve through gap.gaps_dir), so no design conclusion changed.
